MINI MINUTES:

Scott A. Freedman, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Barry Perkins and Nuchanart Perkins

NO APPEARANCE OTHER . TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Barry Perkin's motion to compel attendance at deposition.
The motion is moot in light of the Court's ruling on Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions.

Notice Of Motion For Award Of Monetary Sanctions And To Strike Answer Or Terminating Sanctions (Per Commissioner Chan In Phone Coversation W/ Clerk In 633)
Off Calendar Nov-14-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Scott A. Freedman, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Barry Perkins and Nuchanart Perkins

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Barry Perkin's motion for terminating sanctions is Granted. No opposition has been filed. The Court finds that further orders would not result in compliance. The request for additional monetary sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

RULING: The Court granted the motion. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demand, Set One And For An Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-04-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE:
Scott A. Freedman, Esq. appearing for plaintiff/moving party
No other appearance on this matter.

RULING:
Plaintiff Barry Perkin's and Nuchanart Perkin's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents -- Tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion is granted. Defendant is ordered to serve verified supplemental responses and produce documents within 21 days of notice of this order. Defendant is ordered to pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $365 within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
